calendar.matters:
(Respondent Ming Chao Liu) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Arbitration Award And Request For Order Restraining Flick Inc. From Asserting Unsupported Fraud Claims
Off Calendar May-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: RESPONDENT MING LIU'S (Respondent Ming Chao Liu) Motion To Seal Arbitration Award And Request For Order Restraining Flick Inc. From Asserting Unsupported Fraud Claims.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

David H.S. Commins, Esq., of Commins, Knudsen & Chou, P.C., (510) 823-2208, for Petitioner.

Ming Chao Liu, in propria persona, Respondent.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling.
Respondent Ming Chao Liu's motion to seal arbitration award and request for restraining order is denied. Records of litigation are presumptively public; the court must make specific factual findings to seal them. (See Rule of Court 2.550(d).) Respondent provides no factual support on which the court could make factual findings to seal the record. Moreover, in this case, the arbitration award that was confirmed by the court is the basis for a judgment against Respondent. It is hard to imagine what adequate basis would underlie sealing an entire arbitration award that forms the basis for a public judgment. The request to seal the arbitration award is denied. Respondent also seeks a restraining order to prevent Petitioner Flick Inc. "from publicly asserting or presenting fraud allegations not supported by legal evidence." Respondent provides no legal authority and this court has no freestanding power to enjoin speech or prevent parties from making litigation claims. The court denies this request as well.

An order is submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Flick Inc.'S Motion To Compel On Dec 16 2024 Discovery Hearing
Off Calendar Apr-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
David Commins, Esq. 415-391-6490 Commins & Knudsen for Petitioner.
Ming Liu Respondent 650-678-1589 in Pro Per.

ARGUED. Respondent Mr. Liu contests the tentative ruling and presents arguments. Petitioner's counsel Mr. Commins states sanctions have not been made. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT MING LIU's Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Flick Inc.'S Motion To Compel On Dec 16 2024 Discovery Hearing.

Respondent Ming Chao Liu's Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Flick Inc.'s Motion To Compel On Dec 16, 2024, Discovery Hearing is DENIED.

Respondent fails to identify a basis for her motion. Liberally construed, the motion appears to be a motion for reconsideration. Respondent's motion is denied as procedurally infirm. Respondent has not shown that he made his motion within ten days of service of notice of entry of the challenged order. (See Code Civil Procedure, section 1008(a).) Further, a party seeking reconsideration must demonstrate diligence. (Baldwin v. Home Savings of America (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 1192, 1200.) Respondent here has not done so. As an independent grounds for denial, the court finds the motion it without substantive support. Respondent fails to demonstrate "new or different facts, circumstances, or law" to warrant reconsideration. (See Code Civil Procedure, section 1008(a).)

Mr. Commins is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org by the end of business today.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MINI MINUTES:
Defendant Ming Chao Liu contested the Court's tenative ruling late. Nocite of conteste received by email at 4:21 p.m. Court does not call matter.

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT MING LIU's Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Flick Inc.'s Motion To Compel On December 16, 2024.

Continued to March 26, 2025, and transferred to department 302 at 9:00 a.m. to be heard by Judge Quinn, who ruled on the underlying motion to compel discovery on 12/16/24.

Notice Of Motions And Motions To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Respondent Ming Chao Liu And Supporting Memo
Off Calendar Dec-16-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing re: PETITIONER FLICK, INC's Motions To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Respondent Ming Chao Liu And Supporting Memo.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner Flick Inc.'s Motions To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Respondent Ming Chao Liu is GRANTED. Petitioner's request for sanctions is GRANTED IN PART. Good cause appearing, the court ORDERS Respondent Ming Chao Liu: (1) to provide Code compliant responses to Flick's First Set of Special Interrogatories, and (2) provide a Code compliant response including an appropriate document production, to Flick's First Requests for Production of Documents. Complete responses within 20 days of notice of entry of this order.

The court cannot find Respondent Ming Chao Liu's response (or non-response) to the subject discovery requests and instant motion substantially justified. The court ORDERS Respondent Ming Chao Liu to pay to Flick, Inc. $2,300 as reasonable sanctions. Payment within 20 days of notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (301/JMQ)
